chapter_title.ar: باب عِتْقِ السَّكْرَانِ وَ الْمَجْنُونِ وَ الْمُكْرَهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – Emancipation of the intoxicated, and the insane, and the coerced

text: وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ وَ بُرَيْدِ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ وَ فُضَيْلٍ وَ إِسْمَاعِيلَ الْأَزْرَقِ وَ مَعْمَرِ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّ الْمُدَلَّهَ لَيْسَ عِتْقُهُ بِعِتْقٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad, from Umar Bin Aizna, from Zurara, or said, and Muhammad Bin Muslim, and Bureyd Bin Muawiya, and Fuzeyl, and Ismail Al Azraq, and Ma’amar Bin Yahya,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> and Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that the bewitched, his emancipation is not with a (valid) emancipation’.
